mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, which directly drives corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon. The signal is monotonic at all observable values.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS on 20d forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, the economy has entered a recessionary or late-cycle phase with rising joblessness that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, the proportion of positive 60d forward returns exceeds 50%, or the average return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous combination of elevated consumer price inflation and a high federal funds rate signals that monetary policy is aggressively restrictive, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin pressure, producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined z-score interaction term (CPIAUCSL * FEDFUNDS) has a rank IC below -0.03 over any 2-year window, or the univariate signals are not jointly significant in a linear regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accommodative monetary policy (federal funds rate below 3%), rising housing starts reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which boosts economic growth and equity prices within a short horizon.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, an increase in HOUST does not produce positive 5d returns in more than 60% of cases, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 0.5% indicates the economy is expanding at a healthy pace, driving corporate earnings expectations upward and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 0.5%, the subsequent 60d forward return is positive in less than 55% of instances with available GDP releases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 160,000 signal a robust labor market with rising income and consumption, which supports corporate earnings and pushes equity prices higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS exceeds 160,000, the proportion of positive 20d forward returns falls below 60% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of strong job creation (PAYEMS above 150k) and low unemployment (UNRATE below 5%) reflects a tight labor market that boosts aggregate demand and corporate profits, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When the joint condition is met, the average 20d forward return is negative or not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 100 (the 2017 base) signals that manufacturing output is expanding in real terms, indicating a growing economy that drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 100, the proportion of positive 20d forward returns is below 60% over any rolling 3-year window.
